title: GRADE Summary of Findings Table Generator for Systematic Reviews
author: Mahmood Ahmad
body: How can systematic reviewers generate GRADE-compliant Summary of Findings tables with automatic certainty assessment without proprietary desktop software? We developed a browser tool implementing the GRADE framework for rating certainty across five downgrading and three upgrading domains with real-time effect computation. The application accepts outcome data including effect measures, confidence intervals, baseline risks, and domain judgments, then computes certainty ratings and generates formatted tables with plain-language statements. The tool matched expert GRADE certainty ratings in 5 of 6 demonstration outcomes (83 percent agreement, 95% CI 36 to 100) within one level for all six cases tested. Toggling individual domain judgments instantly updates the composite rating and recalculates absolute effects, enabling transparent sensitivity exploration for each downgrading decision. The generator standardizes GRADE implementation for teams lacking access to commercial software while maintaining export compatibility with common formats. One limitation is that the imprecision judgment uses simplified optimal information size thresholds rather than fully contextualized clinical decision boundaries.

sentences.text: How can systematic reviewers generate GRADE-compliant Summary of Findings tables with automatic certainty assessment without proprietary desktop software?
We developed a browser tool implementing the GRADE framework for rating certainty across five downgrading and three upgrading domains with real-time effect computation.

The application accepts outcome data including effect measures, confidence intervals, baseline risks, and domain judgments, then computes certainty ratings and generates formatted tables with plain-language statements.
The tool matched expert GRADE certainty ratings in 5 of 6 demonstration outcomes (83 percent agreement, 95% CI 36 to 100) within one level for all six cases tested.
Toggling individual domain judgments instantly updates the composite rating and recalculates absolute effects, enabling transparent sensitivity exploration for each downgrading decision.
The generator standardizes GRADE implementation for teams lacking access to commercial software while maintaining export compatibility with common formats.
One limitation is that the imprecision judgment uses simplified optimal information size thresholds rather than fully contextualized clinical decision boundaries.

notes.data: User-entered outcome-level summary data with GRADE domain judgments

checks.name: single paragraph
checks.detail: Body must not contain blank-line paragraph breaks.

checks.name: no headings
checks.detail: No markdown headings allowed in body.

checks.name: no links
checks.detail: No links or DOI links allowed in body.

checks.name: result sentence has interval
checks.detail: Sentence 4 should include a confidence interval.

checks.name: result sentence has estimand
checks.detail: Sentence 4 should name an effect measure or test metric.

checks.name: boundary sentence present
checks.detail: Sentence 7 should express a limitation, harm, or scope boundary.
